prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. The point estimate incorporates a daily drift calculated from an assumed annual return of 8% (typical for a broad market index over the long term). The confidence intervals are derived from an assumed daily volatility of 1.2% over the 18-day period. These parameters (drift and volatility) are estimates based on general market behavior for a stable large-cap stock like MCD, as real-time market data was not accessed. The Z-scores corresponding to the 50%, 80%, and 90% confidence levels were used to establish the upper and lower bounds for the price range.
